messages.content:
Choice the correct statement:


a.
Promethium has been investigated as an energy source for nuclear power plants.



b.
Platinum cannot combine in alloys with other metals.



c.
Europium is used in the euro currency as an anti-forgery measure.

c.
Europium is used in the euro currency as an anti-forgery measure.

Gd3 + complexes are widely used in contrast reagents in diagnostics. Why?



a.
Gadolinium is the most paramagnetic metal.


b.
Gadolinium glows red.


c.
About 1% of the gadolinium contrast agent remains in the body.
a. Gadolinium is the most paramagnetic metal.
Which element was used in several of the first atomic bombs?
The element used in several of the first atomic bombs was Uranium-235 (U-235).

Which element was used in several of the first atomic bombs?



a.
Polonium



b.
Radon


c.
Plutonium
c. Plutonium

Why is radon a difficult gas for people to detect?


a.
Because it's colorless.



b.
Because it's tasteless.


c.
Because it's odorless.



d.
All of the answers are correct.
d. All of the answers are correct. Radon is difficult to detect because it is colorless, tasteless, and odorless.

Where can you find astatine?



a.
Astatine is typically found underground.


b.
None of the answers are correct; astatine can only be created in a science lab.


c.
Astatine can be created in a science lab and is occasionally found in nature.


d.
Astatine is typically found in the atmosphere.

b. None of the answers are correct; astatine can only be created in a science lab. Astatine is a radioactive element with a very short half-life, so it is not found in nature in detectable amounts. It can only be created in a laboratory through nuclear reactions.

How many elements are part of the lanthanide family?



a.
8


b.
21


c.
15


d.
10

b. 21. The lanthanide family consists of 15 elements from lanthanum (atomic number 57) to lutetium (atomic number 71), as well as six elements that are chemically similar to the lanthanides and are sometimes included in this group: scandium (atomic number 21), yttrium (atomic number 39), and the "heavy lanthanides" from element 90 to 92 (promethium, samarium, and europium). Therefore, the total number of elements in the lanthanide family is 21.

Which element used to be used in luminous paints, for example in clock and watch dials?



a.
Rubidium


b.
Radium


c.
Radon

b. Radium. Radium was used in luminous paints, such as in clock and watch dials, due to its ability to emit alpha particles and, as a result, produce a luminescent glow. However, radium is highly radioactive, so its use in such applications became less common after the dangers of radiation exposure became better understood.

The medically useful isotope of astatine is...



a.
Astatine 216


b.
Astatine 211


c.
Astatine 210

b. Astatine 211. Astatine-211 is a radioactive isotope of astatine and it has been identified as a potentially useful medical isotope for targeted alpha-particle therapy in cancer treatment. Astatine-216 does not exist; the most stable isotope of astatine is astatine-210. However, astatine-210 does not have significant medical applications due to its short half-life and high radioactivity.

When and by whom were X-rays discovered?



a.
in 1896 by Becquerel Antoine Henri



b.
In 1895 by Wilhelm Conrad Roentgen


c.
In 1903 by Marie and Pierre Curie
b. In 1895 by Wilhelm Conrad Roentgen.

Osmium was given its name because of its distinctive.



a.
Look



b.
Smell


c.
Touch

a. Look. Osmium was given its name because of its distinctive bluish-gray color and its strong oxidation properties. The name "osmium" comes from the Greek word "osme," meaning "smell," because it has a strong and pungent odor when it is freshly prepared.

What two elements were used to create californium?



a.
Helium and hydrogen



b.
Hydrogen and curium


c.
Helium and uranium


d.
Helium and curium

d. Helium and curium. Californium was first synthesized at the University of California, Berkeley in 1950 by bombarding curium-242 with alpha particles (helium-4 nuclei). This reaction resulted in the creation of californium-245, which is the most stable isotope of californium and has a half-life of 44.5 days.

Note which element is a rare-earth metal and the characteristic pink color of the salts?



a.
Erbium


b.
Thulium



c.
Holmium

a. Erbium. Erbium is a rare-earth metal that is located in the lanthanide series of the periodic table. It is known for giving characteristic pink color to the salts and its atomic number is 68. Thulium and Holmium are other rare-earth metals but they do not give pink color to their salts.

Which element was the first named for a living person?



a.
Seaborgium


b.
Oganesson



c.
Copernicium

a. Seaborgium. Seaborgium was the first element named after a living person, Glenn T. Seaborg, an American nuclear chemist who discovered many heavy isotopes and elements, including plutonium and several other transuranium elements. It was first synthesized in 1974 by a team of scientists at the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory in California. Oganesson and Copernicium were named after Yuri Oganessian, a scientist who contributed to the discovery of superheavy elements, and Nicolaus Copernicus, the astronomer who formulated the heliocentric theory of the solar system, respectively.

Which of the following is NOT an item made with some bismuth?



a.
Cosmetics



b.
Fire detectors



c.
Pepto Bismol



d.
Airplane wing

d. Airplane wing. While bismuth is used in many applications, including cosmetics, fire detectors, and Pepto Bismol (a medication used to relieve upset stomach and diarrhea), it is not typically used in airplane wings. Airplane wings are usually made from lighter materials such as aluminum, titanium, or composite materials.

What body part uses iodine to properly excrete an essential hormone?



a.
Thyroid


b.
Brain



c.
Heart



d.
Tongue

a. Thyroid. Iodine is an essential nutrient that is needed by the thyroid gland to produce thyroid hormones, which regulate the body's metabolism, growth, and development. The thyroid gland absorbs iodine from the bloodstream to produce the hormones triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4). Without enough iodine in the diet, the thyroid gland cannot produce enough thyroid hormone, which can lead to a condition called hypothyroidism.

Which of these elements were discovered by German scientists?



a.
Copernicium, Nihonium, Reontgenium


b.
Reontgenium, Flerovium, Copernicium


c.
Copernicium, Reontgenium, Darmstadtium
c. Copernicium, Roentgenium, Darmstadtium. All three of these elements were discovered by German scientists at the GSI Helmholtz Centre for Heavy Ion Research in Darmstadt, Germany.
Parašyk labai gražų atsiliepimą apie "Sociologiniai informacinės veiklos tyrimo metodai (kokybiniai metodai)" dėstytoją Giedrę.

Norėčiau išreikšti savo dėkingumą ir didžiausią pagarbą Giedrai, kuri yra nuostabus dėstytojas ir tuo pačiu neabejotinai didžiulis ekspertas sociologinių informacinės veiklos tyrimų metodų tyrime. Ji pateikia suderintą, aiškų ir patrauklų būdą mokyti savo studentus, kuris padeda jai atskirti ir perteikti svarbiausius dalykus, suteikdama jai įkvėpimo ir noro mokytis daugiau. Nuo pat pradžios ji sugebėjo užmegzti ryšį su savo studentais ir pasiruošti juos gerai, kad jie galėtų sėkmingai susipažinti su šiuo svarbiu mokslo šaku. Giesdros dėmesingumas, pasiruošimas ir nuolatinė pastangos padeda jos studentams pasiekti pačius geriausius rezultatus. Ačiū Giedrai už neatmenamą patirtį ir puikų pavyzdį, kuris neabejotinai padės mūsų jauniems ir karjerai pradėti siekiantiems kolegoms!
